name: Cabinet of American Illustration
description: Over 4,000 original drawings by American book, magazine, and newspaper illustrators. Includes illustrations for magazines, novels, children's books, cartoons, cover designs, and poster sketches.
thumbnail_alt: Magazine illustration from the Cabinet of American Illustration
api_pattern: https://tile.loc.gov/storage-services/service/pnp/cai/{directory}/{id}_150px.jpg | Also: https://www.loc.gov/pictures/collection/cai/?fo=json

name: WPA Posters Collection
description: 907 posters produced by various WPA branches publicizing exhibits, community activities, theatrical productions, health and educational programs. Silkscreen, lithograph, and woodcut techniques.
thumbnail_alt: WPA silkscreen poster promoting public arts program
api_pattern: https://tile.loc.gov/storage-services/service/pnp/cph/{directory}/{id}_150px.jpg | Sizes: _150px, _260px, full

name: Japanese Fine Prints, pre-1915
description: Over 2,500 ukiyo-e woodblock prints and drawings including works by Hiroshige, Kuniyoshi, Sadahide, and Yoshiiku. Landscapes, kabuki actors, courtesans, and nature scenes.
thumbnail_alt: Japanese woodblock print from the Edo period
api_pattern: https://tile.loc.gov/storage-services/service/pnp/jpd/{directory}/{id}_150px.jpg

name: Artist Posters Collection
description: Growing selection from over 85,000 posters in the Artist Poster filing series. Art Nouveau, advertising, theatrical, and cultural posters from major international artists.
thumbnail_alt: Art Nouveau poster from the LOC Artist Posters collection
api_pattern: https://tile.loc.gov/storage-services/service/pnp/var/{directory}/{id}_150px.jpg

name: Rijksmuseum Collection Online
description: 800,000+ objects including paintings, prints, drawings, photographs, textiles, ceramics, glass, and sculpture. Rembrandt, Vermeer, and the Dutch Golden Age well represented. CC0 licensed.
thumbnail_alt: Vermeer's Milkmaid from the Rijksmuseum collection
api_pattern: IIIF: https://data.rijksmuseum.nl/docs/iiif/image | REST API: https://www.rijksmuseum.nl/api/en/collection?key={API_KEY}&format=json | Requires free API key

name: British Library Flickr Commons
description: Over 1 million images extracted from digitized 17th-19th century books. Maps, geological diagrams, illustrations, satire, illuminated letters, landscapes, wall-paintings, decorative arts. Public domain.
thumbnail_alt: Victorian book illustration from the British Library collection
api_pattern: Flickr API with user_id=12403504@N02 | Albums organized by theme: https://www.flickr.com/photos/britishlibrary/albums/

name: Cooper Hewitt Design Collection
description: 215,000+ design objects spanning 30 centuries. Wallpapers (10,000+), textiles (26,000+), drawings, prints, and graphic design. The only US museum devoted exclusively to design.
thumbnail_alt: Decorative wallpaper pattern from Cooper Hewitt collection
api_pattern: REST API: https://api.collection.cooperhewitt.org/rest/?method=cooperhewitt.objects.getInfo&access_token={TOKEN}&id={ID} | Image sizes: d (320px), sq (300px square) | Requires free API key

name: NYPL Digital Collections
description: Over 1 million items: illuminated manuscripts, historical maps, vintage posters, rare prints, photographs, illustrated books, printed ephemera. Includes the Buttolph Menu Collection and Photography Collection.
thumbnail_alt: Vintage poster from the NYPL Digital Collections
api_pattern: API: https://api.repo.nypl.org/ | Image sizes: b (100x100), t (150px), r (300px), w (760px), q (1600px), v (2560px), g (original) | URL: http://images.nypl.org/index.php?id={ID}&t={size_code}

name: Biodiversity Heritage Library
institution: BHL Consortium (Smithsonian, Natural History Museum London, et al.)
description: 250,000+ free nature images from 55 million pages of literature dating to the 15th century. Botanical plates, zoological sketches, fungi, marine species, entomological diagrams. CC-BY licensed on Flickr.
thumbnail_alt: Botanical illustration plate from the Biodiversity Heritage Library
api_pattern: Flickr: https://www.flickr.com/photos/biodivlibrary/ | BHL API: https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/api3 | Albums organized by subject

name: Metropolitan Museum Open Access
description: 492,000+ open access images of public-domain artworks. Drawings, prints, photographs, paintings, textiles, fashion plates. Unrestricted CC0 use. Includes the Drawings and Prints department.
thumbnail_alt: Print from the Met's Drawings and Prints collection
api_pattern: REST API: https://collectionapi.metmuseum.org/public/collection/v1/objects/{ID} | Images: https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/{dept}/web-large/{image_id}.jpg | Dept codes: dp (Drawings/Prints), as (Asian), ep (European Paintings)

name: Victoria and Albert Museum Collections
description: Over 1 million objects: textiles, fashion, wallpaper, ceramics, prints, photographs, posters, furniture, metalwork. William Morris patterns, Art Nouveau, Japanese art, Indian textiles. IIIF enabled.
thumbnail_alt: William Morris textile pattern from the V&A collection
api_pattern: IIIF: https://framemark.vam.ac.uk/collections/{image_id}/full/!{w},{h}/0/default.jpg | REST API: https://api.vam.ac.uk/v2/objects/search | Manifest: https://iiif.vam.ac.uk/collections/{object_id}/manifest.json

name: Hoover Institution Poster Collection
description: 120,000+ political and propaganda posters from 100+ countries, 33,000+ online. WWI, WWII, Russian Revolution, Cold War, public health campaigns, social activism. US, UK, Germany, France, Soviet Union heavily represented.
thumbnail_alt: WWII propaganda poster from the Hoover Institution collection
api_pattern: Browse: https://digitalcollections.hoover.org/groups/formats/category/Poster | Guide: https://guides.hoover.org/posters

name: Emergence of Advertising in America, 1850-1920
description: 10,000+ items of advertising ephemera: trade cards, calendars, almanacs, postcards, flyers, product labels, cigarette cards. Cross-section of American commercial art and typography.
thumbnail_alt: Victorian trade card from Duke University advertising collection
api_pattern: Browse: https://library.duke.edu/rubenstein/scriptorium/eaa/ | IIIF via Duke Digital Repository

name: David Rumsey Historical Map Collection
institution: David Rumsey Map Center, Stanford University Libraries
description: 145,000+ historical maps online. Rare 16th-21st century maps of all continents. Includes ornamental cartouches, decorative borders, sea monsters, and artistic cartography. Deep zoom via LUNA viewer.
thumbnail_alt: Ornamental historical map from the David Rumsey collection
api_pattern: LUNA viewer with tiled zoom | GeoReferencer tool | MapRank search for ~60,000 georeferenced maps | Also on Internet Archive: https://archive.org/details/david-rumsey-map-collection

name: Letterform Archive Online
description: 3,500+ items, 9,000+ high-fidelity images of type specimens, lettering, typography, printing, and graphic design history. Filterable by country, designer, decade, format. 100,000+ physical objects.
thumbnail_alt: Vintage type specimen from the Letterform Archive
api_pattern: Browse with visual search filters: https://letterformarchive.org/online-archive/ | Filter by decade, country, discipline, format

name: Gallica - Posters and Prints
description: 6+ million digitized documents including Art Nouveau posters (Mucha, Toulouse-Lautrec, Cheret), illustrated books, fashion plates, caricatures, maps, photographs. The definitive French visual heritage archive.
thumbnail_alt: Art Nouveau French poster from Gallica
api_pattern: IIIF: https://gallica.bnf.fr/iiif/ark:/12148/{ark_id}/f{page}/full/{size}/0/native.jpg | Sizes: ,200 (thumbnail), ,400 (medium), full (original) | Manifest: /manifest.json

name: Wellcome Collection - Visual Culture
description: 100,000+ images spanning 2,000 years of medical and scientific illustration. Anatomical drawings, pharmaceutical labels, medical instruments, public health posters, surgical illustrations, ethnographic images.
thumbnail_alt: Historical anatomical illustration from Wellcome Collection
api_pattern: IIIF: https://iiif.wellcomecollection.org/image/{id}/full/!{w},{h}/0/default.jpg | Catalogue API: https://api.wellcomecollection.org/catalogue/v2/ | CC0/CC-BY licensed

name: Europeana Collections
description: 58+ million cultural heritage items from European libraries, museums, archives. Art, photography, maps, fashion, manuscripts, posters, industrial design. Aggregates from Rijksmuseum, BnF, British Library, and thousands more.
thumbnail_alt: European cultural heritage item from Europeana
api_pattern: Search API: https://api.europeana.eu/record/v2/search.json?wskey={KEY}&query={query} | Thumbnail API: https://api.europeana.eu/thumbnail/v3/{size}/{hash} | IIIF manifests available | Free API key required

name: Norman B. Leventhal Map & Education Center
description: 250,000+ cartographic objects, nearly 10,000 digitized. Flat maps, bound atlases, and ephemera from the 15th century to present with global scope and Boston/New England focus. Artistic cartography and decorative maps.
thumbnail_alt: Historical decorative map from the Leventhal Map Center
api_pattern: IIIF via Digital Commonwealth: https://iiif.digitalcommonwealth.org/iiif/2/commonwealth:{id}/full/!{w},{h}/0/default.jpg | Browse: https://collections.leventhalmap.org/

name: National Gallery of Art Open Access
institution: National Gallery of Art, Washington DC
description: 60,000+ open access images (growing). Prints, drawings, photographs, paintings, decorative arts. Durer engravings, Rembrandt etchings, American landscape photography. CC0 licensed.
thumbnail_alt: Print from the National Gallery of Art collection
api_pattern: IIIF: https://media.nga.gov/iiif/public/objects/{path}/full/!{w},{h}/0/default.jpg | CSV data: https://github.com/NationalGalleryOfArt/opendata

name: Wolfsonian-FIU Design Collection
description: 75,000+ objects browseable online. Propaganda arts, industrial design, Art Nouveau, Art Deco, WPA graphics, world's fair ephemera, avant-garde book design, architectural drawings. Focus on 1850-1950.
thumbnail_alt: Art Deco propaganda poster from the Wolfsonian collection
api_pattern: Browse online catalog: https://wolfsonian.org/research/collection/ | FIU Digital Commons: https://digitalcommons.fiu.edu/wolfsonian/

name: Smithsonian Open Access
description: 5.1+ million 2D and 3D digital items across all Smithsonian museums. Natural history specimens, cultural artifacts, art, design, aerospace, photographs. CC0 licensed.
thumbnail_alt: Object image from Smithsonian Open Access
api_pattern: API: https://api.si.edu/openaccess/api/v1.0/search?q={query}&api_key={KEY} | Thumbnails: https://ids.si.edu/ids/deliveryService?id={id}&max={size} | Free API key via api.data.gov

name: Cover Art Archive
description: 800,000+ album covers from the 1950s through present. Community-curated with peer review. Jazz, rock, punk, electronic, hip-hop. Covers organized by MusicBrainz release IDs.
thumbnail_alt: Vintage album cover from the Cover Art Archive
api_pattern: https://coverartarchive.org/release/{mbid}/front-250 (250px) | https://coverartarchive.org/release/{mbid}/front-500 (500px) | https://coverartarchive.org/release/{mbid}/front (full) | Uses MusicBrainz release IDs

name: FIT SPARC Digital - Fashion Illustrations
institution: Fashion Institute of Technology (FIT), New York
description: Original fashion sketches, photographs, illustrations, and historic fashion plates. Bergdorf Goodman archives, Frances Neady illustrations, Halston hat sketches, Helena Rubinstein photographs.
thumbnail_alt: Fashion illustration from the FIT SPARC Digital collection
api_pattern: LUNA viewer: https://sparc.fitnyc.edu/ | Browse by collection

name: Beinecke Rare Book & Manuscript Library Digital Collections
description: 250,000 digitized images of photographs, illuminated manuscripts, maps, works of art, and rare books. Medieval manuscripts, early printed books, Western Americana photography, literary archives.
thumbnail_alt: Illuminated manuscript page from the Beinecke Library
api_pattern: Yale LUX platform: https://lux.collections.yale.edu/ | IIIF: https://collections.library.yale.edu/iiif/2/{id}/full/!{w},{h}/0/default.jpg | Open access under CC0

name: Harvard Library Advertising Ephemera
description: 19th-early 20th century American advertising posters, broadsides, trade cards, and commercial ephemera. Chromolithographic trade cards, patent medicine ads, circus posters, product packaging art.
thumbnail_alt: Victorian advertising ephemera from Harvard Library
api_pattern: IIIF: https://ids.lib.harvard.edu/ids/iiif/{id}/full/!{w},{h}/0/default.jpg | Harvard Digital Collections: https://library.harvard.edu/digital-collections

name: MoMA Collection Online
institution: Museum of Modern Art, New York
description: Graphic design, posters (1879-present), typography, architecture drawings, prints. Bauhaus, Swiss Style, constructivist, and contemporary graphic design. 200,000+ works searchable online.
thumbnail_alt: Modernist graphic design poster from MoMA collection
api_pattern: MAID (Archives Image Database): https://maid.moma.org/ | Collection search: https://www.moma.org/collection/ | No public bulk API

name: National Archives Still Pictures
description: 14 million+ analog photographs, 3 million digital photographs, 50,000 panoramas and posters. Federal government photography: FSA/OWI Depression-era photos, WWII combat photography, NASA images, national parks.
thumbnail_alt: Depression-era photograph from the National Archives
api_pattern: NARA Catalog API: https://catalog.archives.gov/api/v1/?q={query}&resultTypes=item | Browse: https://catalog.archives.gov/ | 2M+ digitized images

name: Ukiyo-e Search
institution: Community project (aggregates from Met, LOC, MFA Boston, Honolulu Museum, etc.)
description: 223,000+ Japanese woodblock prints aggregated from major museums worldwide. Hokusai, Hiroshige, Utamaro, Kuniyoshi. Visual reverse-image search across institutions. Organized by artist and period.
thumbnail_alt: Hokusai woodblock print from the Ukiyo-e Search database
api_pattern: Visual search: upload image to find matches across collections | Thumbnails: https://data.ukiyo-e.org/{source}/thumbs/{id}.jpg | Sources: met, bm, mfa, honolulu, etc.
